disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Rapidly progressive facial swelling over the past 12 hours
trigger_question_examples: How quickly has the swelling in your face developed?
Is the swelling getting bigger over the last several hours?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's gotten a lot worse just over the last 12 hours, my cheek is really puffed up now.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Limited jaw opening (trismus) to less than 2cm
trigger_question_examples: How far can you open your mouth right now?
Is it hard to open your jaw fully?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I can barely open my mouth, maybe less than the width of a finger.

trigger_question_examples: Do you feel feverish or generally unwell?
Have you taken your temperature or do you feel chills/sweats?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Yes, I feel hot and generally awful all over.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No difficulty swallowing saliva, no drooling, no noisy or difficult breathing currently
trigger_question_examples: Are you able to swallow your own saliva normally?
Any noisy breathing, drooling, or trouble breathing?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No, I can swallow fine and my breathing feels normal so far.

patient_initiated_exchanges.patient_text: Can I just get antibiotics prescribed over the phone instead of going anywhere tonight?
patient_initiated_exchanges.expected_ai_doctor_response_quality: Should explain that rapidly progressive facial swelling with trismus (limited jaw opening) suggests infection is spreading beyond the tooth into the surrounding soft tissues, with a risk of progression to a deep neck space infection that can threaten the airway — this requires urgent in-person assessment tonight (ED or urgent dental/maxillofacial service) for examination, likely IV antibiotics, and monitoring, not a phone-only antibiotic prescription.
